what_it_does: A companion findings doc that tiers the candidate semantic detectors a 4B swarm could run (half-migration, intent-vs-implementation drift, concept/vocabulary coherence, doc contradiction/staleness, semantic coverage mapping). For each it specifies the read-unit, the real artifacts read, a Pydantic schema/prompt shape, the false-positive risk under positive-only discipline, and an honest verdict of feasible-now / candidate-only / not-reliable. It stress-tests against two named incidents (the /status half-migration and the mode-dial-built-twice).
observations: Central thesis: a 4B is a good adjudicator of a candidate pair but a poor discoverer of one, so relational detectors need a two-stage pairing-then-adjudication architecture.
Grounds the engine's shape in cognition.py:554-558 — run_swarm fires independent roles each reading only ctx.utterance, a map over independent units, not a join.
Provides concrete Pydantic output_schema + ROLE dicts per detector, modeled on roles/check.py.

Tiers the detectors: doc-staleness and shallow intent-drift FEASIBLE-NOW; deep intent-drift NOT-RELIABLE; concept-coherence CANDIDATE-ONLY-and-gated; half-migration CANDIDATE-ONLY; semantic coverage NOT-RELIABLE.

Corrects the /status framing: the /api/mockup-feedback/status handler still exists at bridge.py:1564 with the full pending|applied|dismissed lifecycle; it is a live coexistence + lifecycle-asymmetry, not a deletion.
Notes the fabric can call embeddings (transport.py:196-217) but no embedder is registered and no clustering primitive exists, so the concept-coherence pre-filter has no in-Company home.
Cites verify_jury.py:12-14 that N draws on one model are correlated samples measuring variance not independent error.
standouts: The two-stage diagram: a cheap deterministic pairing pre-stage curates read-units, the swarm adjudicates them; relational detectors are gated on the pre-stage.
The correction that framing the half-migration as coexistence (not deletion) makes the detector MORE buildable since it needs no git/history awareness.
The confidence-self-declaration field routing low-confidence dataflow judgments to candidate-only by construction.
conventions: Anchors every detector to real file:line artifacts and a closed-vocabulary schema.
Holds the positive-only law as the backstop and notes its inverted failure mode (false-wrong/cry-wolf) vs the structural layer's false-dead.
notes: If briefing someone: this is the per-detector honesty table — two of five feasible now, two candidate-only-and-gated, one not-reliable — built on the insight that the structural layer's flag is the semantic layer's read-unit.
questions: Where does the net-new embedding-clustering pairing primitive live, given the doc says it has no in-Company home?
How is the generalized run_role ctx-to-messages mapping implemented to feed a file pair instead of an utterance?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches its stated area: it enumerates and tiers the §4① detectors with read-units, schemas, FP-risk and verdicts, stress-tested against the two named incidents.
apparent_themes: Honest tiering over monolithic capability claims
intention_signals: It is trying to convert a list of hoped-for detectors into a buildable, honestly-tiered design, making explicit which detectors ride the flat swarm directly and which require a pairing pre-stage the swarm cannot provide.
novelty: The distinct pairing/adjudication split and the coexistence-not-deletion correction to the /status incident are particular contributions, as is the inverted-failure-mode framing of positive-only.
contribution: Contributes the per-detector tiering, concrete schemas/prompts, the pairing-pre-stage requirement for relational detectors, and the grounded re-reading of both named incidents as live coexistence rather than deletion.
summary_for_embedding: A findings doc tiering five candidate 4B semantic detectors by feasibility with read-units, schemas, and FP-risk, arguing relational detectors need a candidate-pairing pre-stage the flat swarm cannot supply.
intention_for_embedding: To determine which meaning-level coherence checks a 4B swarm can feasibly run and how far each can be trusted.
